title: Ternary Operator
description: Shorten simple if-else with a compact syntax.

slides.title: Ternary: Shorthand if-else
slides.content:
# condition ? value_if_true : value_if_false

The ternary operator is a compact way to write simple if-else.

```php
$result = ($age >= 18) ? "Adult" : "Minor";
```

It evaluates the condition, returns the first expression if true, else the second.

slides.title: Ternary Examples
Don't Nest Ternary
slides.content:
## Avoid Stacking Ternaries

Nesting ternary operators can be very confusing. If you need complex logic, use if-else or switch.

```php
// This is hard to read!
$result = ($a > $b) ? ($a > $c ? $a : $c) : ($b > $c ? $b : $c);
```

Better to use `max($a, $b, $c)` or if statements.

quizData.question:
What does this code output?

```php
echo (5 > 3) ? "Yes" : "No";
```
